skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Image, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input. Triggers: nano banana, gemini image, gemini 3 pro image, gemini 2.5 flash image, google image generation, native image generation, gemini native image
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Im...
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. This skill provides documentation with example bash commands for the inference.sh CLI tool. External commands are example commands users run themselves in their terminal, not code executed by the skill. Network URLs are legitimate documentation links. The skill is safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Users need an easy way to create AI images using Google Gemini models directly from their terminal. This skill provides commands and examples for generating images with Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 2.5 Flash via the inference.sh CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Gemini 3 Pro Image model
Generate images using the faster Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model
Edit existing images by providing input images and prompt instructions
Control output with aspect ratio, resolution, and number of images
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time information in images
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Requires API credits or subscription with inference.sh
Image generation is subject to model-specific content policies

use_cases.title: Create AI Illustrations
use_cases.description: Generate custom illustrations for projects, presentations, or creative work using text prompts

use_cases.title: Edit Photos with AI
use_cases.description: Modify existing images by describing changes you want the AI to make

use_cases.title: Quick Prototyping
use_cases.description: Rapidly generate concept art and visual mockups during product development

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to generate a simple image using the Gemini 2.5 Flash model with the prompt 'a red apple on a wooden table'

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 logo concepts for a tech startup using Gemini 3 Pro

prompt_templates.title: Custom Aspect Ratio
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landscape image with aspect ratio 16:9 showing a mountain at sunset

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to edit an image by adding a rainbow to a landscape photo

output_examples.output: The skill provides the command: infsh app run google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview --input '{"prompt": "a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars"}'
Shows how to use num_images parameter to generate multiple variations in one command
Demonstrates providing an images array with the prompt to modify an existing image

best_practices: Be specific in your prompts - include details about style, lighting, composition, and mood
Use Gemini 2.5 Flash for faster results when prototyping or iterating on ideas
Use Gemini 3 Pro for final outputs requiring the highest quality
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make something cool' - be specific about your vision
Do not skip the login step - you must authenticate with inference.sh first
Do not exceed the maximum of 14 input images when editing

faq.question: What models does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports Gemini 3 Pro Image (highest quality) and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image (fastest) via inference.sh

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the inference.sh CLI and authenticate with your account. Credits or a subscription are required.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide up to 14 input images along with a prompt describing the changes you want.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the num_images parameter to generate up to 4 images in a single request.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code AI assistants.
